metadata.summary: Choose the right AI video generation provider (Runway, Luma, Sora 2, Veo 3, Replicate, Kling) by matching capabilities to task requirements.

content.title: AI Video Generation Provider Selection
content.summary: Video generation has evolved rapidly with commercial APIs from Runway, Luma, OpenAI Sora, and Google Veo providing production-ready solutions. Select a provider by matching its unique capabilities (lip-sync, native audio, duration, resolution) to task requirements before writing any integration code.

why.p: Each provider excels at a narrow subset of tasks. Runway Gen-3 is the only production API with lip-sync and video-to-video. Sora 2 supports up to 60-second clips. Veo 3 is the only option with native audio generation and 4K resolution. Luma Dream Machine supports video extension. Replicate gives access to open-source models for cost optimization and custom fine-tuning. Choosing the wrong provider either wastes credits on inferior quality or blocks the pipeline entirely when a required capability is absent.

list.item: Selecting a provider before starting any video generation integration.
Evaluating provider capabilities for a new content pipeline (news, social media, marketing).
Switching providers due to API access changes, cost, or quality regression.
Building a multi-provider abstraction and deciding which providers to include.
Provider already selected and integration is underway — refer to async-api or production-service methodologies instead.
Precise character consistency across multiple shots is required — no current provider reliably delivers this; consider human production.
Video longer than 60 seconds from a single call is needed — all current APIs cap at 5-60s per generation; use concat workflow instead.

section.name: provider-comparison
section.p: Providers as of 2025-2026. API status and pricing change frequently — verify before committing.
section.term: Provider, Model, Max Duration, Resolution, API Status, Pricing: Runway Gen-3 Alpha: 10s, 1080p, Public API, $15/mo + credits. Luma AI Dream Machine: 5s extendable, 1080p, Public API, Credits-based. OpenAI Sora 2: 60s, 1080p, Limited API (ChatGPT Pro/Plus), ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription. Google Veo 3: 8s, 4K, Limited API (Vertex AI), Vertex AI enterprise. Replicate (various models): Varies, Varies, Public API, Per-generation. Kling 1.5: 10s, 1080p, Public API, Credits-based.

Feature support matrix (Yes / No / Limited / Good / Excellent): Text-to-video: Runway Yes, Luma Yes, Sora 2 Yes, Veo 3 Yes. Image-to-video: Runway Yes, Luma Yes, Sora 2 Yes, Veo 3 Yes. Video-to-video: Runway Yes, Luma No, Sora 2 Limited, Veo 3 Yes. Video extension: Runway Yes, Luma Yes, Sora 2 No, Veo 3 Yes. Lip-sync: Runway Yes, Luma No, Sora 2 No, Veo 3 No. Native audio: Runway No, Luma No, Sora 2 Yes, Veo 3 Yes. Motion control: Runway Yes, Luma Limited, Sora 2 Limited, Veo 3 Yes. Character consistency: Runway Good, Luma Good, Sora 2 Excellent, Veo 3 Excellent.

rule.statement: Choose Runway Gen-3 when the task requires a reliable production API, video-to-video transformations, lip-sync for talking head videos, or precise motion control.
rule.rationale: Runway is the only provider with a stable public REST API that supports all four of these capabilities simultaneously as of 2026.

rule.statement: Choose Luma Dream Machine when the task requires simple text-to-video workflow, image animation, video extension capabilities, or is budget-conscious.
rule.rationale: Luma's credits-based pricing is typically lower per-second than Runway; it supports video extension which Sora 2 does not.

rule.statement: Choose Sora 2 when highest quality is the priority, long-form content up to 60 seconds is needed, photorealistic output is required, and a ChatGPT Pro subscription is available.
rule.rationale: Sora 2 produces the best character consistency and supports 60-second single-call generation; however, API access is gated to ChatGPT Pro/Plus subscribers as of 2026.

rule.statement: Choose Veo 3 when 4K resolution is required, native audio or dialogue is needed, Google Cloud Vertex AI infrastructure is available, or the use case is enterprise-scale.
rule.rationale: Veo 3 is the only provider with native audio generation; 4K output via Vertex AI is not available as a self-service API to most developers as of 2026.

rule.statement: Choose Replicate when open-source models are needed, custom model fine-tuning is required, cost optimization via self-hosting is planned, or the workflow is experimental.
rule.rationale: Replicate provides access to 50+ open video models (CogVideoX, AnimateDiff, Zeroscope, Stable Video Diffusion) with per-generation pricing and the option to self-host.

section.name: market-context
section.p: Market context (2025-2026): Text-to-video market $0.4B (2025) projected to $1.18B (2029). Global AI video generator market $2.56B by 2032 (19.5% CAGR). Key trends: native audio integration (Sora 2, Veo 3) and 4K resolution becoming standard.

section.name: limitations
section.p: Cross-provider limitations to evaluate before selecting:

list.item: Physics simulation is poor across all providers: liquid, smoke, hands, and complex multi-object interactions degrade quickly.
Character consistency across cuts requires careful prompt repetition and image seeding — still unreliable even in best providers.
Native audio (Sora 2, Veo 3) cannot be controlled: voices, accents, and content are non-deterministic.
4K from Veo 3 requires Vertex AI access — not available as a self-service API to most developers (2026).
Runway Gen-3 lip-sync works only with a frontal face image and clear audio track; off-angle faces fail.
All providers are async: generation takes 60-300s; agent must poll, not block.
Video generation is expensive ($0.05-$0.50 per second of output) — cost modeling is mandatory before production.

checklist.check: Define video requirements: duration, resolution, aspect ratio, audio needs.
Identify required capabilities from the matrix (lip-sync, native audio, video extension, motion control).
Verify API availability and access tier (public vs. enterprise vs. subscription-gated).
Estimate costs based on expected volume at $0.05-$0.50/second.
Plan for async processing (video gen takes 60-300s per clip).
Confirm provider fits content policy for your use case (brand, character, IP).
